target.title: fix(plugins): load dreaming engine when memory slot plugin omitted from allowlist
target.body:
## Summary

- **Problem:** When a third-party Dreaming-compatible memory plugin holds the memory slot and `plugins.allow` is restrictive (omitting `memory-core`, the default dreaming engine), the dreaming engine is rejected as `not-in-allowlist` and never loads, so dreaming sweeps silently never run.
- **Why now:** Reported in #92536 with a source-level repro; affects any user who locks down `plugins.allow` while running dreaming on a non-default memory plugin.
- **Intended outcome:** The bundled dreaming engine activates as a sidecar of the selected memory slot even under a restrictive allowlist — on every registry surface (gateway loader, CLI plugin registry, installed-plugin index, `openclaw plugins list`) — while denylist and `entries.<id>.enabled: false` still win.
- **Out of scope:** No new config/default surface, no change to how dreaming is configured, no gateway-startup changes (that path already includes the dreaming ids unconditionally via `resolveGatewayStartupDreamingPluginIds`).
- **Reviewer focus:** The exemption guard in `resolvePluginActivationDecisionShared` (scoped to bundled origin + restrictive allowlist), and the central derivation of the sidecar id in `resolvePluginActivationState` (`src/plugins/config-state.ts`) instead of per-caller threading.

How it works: `resolveDreamingSidecarEngineId` (moved from `loader.ts` to `config-state.ts`) resolves the bundled dreaming engine id (`memory-core`) only when dreaming is enabled and a different plugin holds the memory slot. `resolvePluginActivationState` passes it to the shared activation decision, which exempts exactly that plugin from the `not-in-allowlist` rejection — only when it is bundled and would otherwise be rejected. The loader's existing memory-slot guards (`pluginId !== dreamingEngineId`) keep `memory-core` from claiming the slot, so it loads strictly as the sidecar.

## Linked context

Closes #92536

Was this requested by a maintainer or owner? No — found via issue triage.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: `memory-core` (the dreaming engine) is wrongly disabled (`not-in-allowlist`) when a restrictive `plugins.allow` omits it while a third-party plugin holds the memory slot with dreaming enabled, so dreaming sweeps never run.
- Real environment tested: Real `openclaw` CLI built from this branch (`pnpm build`, then `pnpm openclaw`), Node 25 on macOS, real bundled `memory-core` and `memory-lancedb` extensions from `dist/extensions`, isolated state via `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=/tmp/openclaw-proof-state` and `OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=/tmp/openclaw-proof-state/openclaw.json`. No mocks, no test runner.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  ```bash
  cat /tmp/openclaw-proof-state/openclaw.json
  # {
  #   "plugins": {
  #     "allow": ["memory-lancedb"],
  #     "slots": { "memory": "memory-lancedb" },
  #     "entries": {
  #       "memory-lancedb": { "enabled": true, "config": { "dreaming": { "enabled": true } } }
  #     }
  #   }
  # }
  pnpm build
  OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=/tmp/openclaw-proof-state \
  OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=/tmp/openclaw-proof-state/openclaw.json \
    pnpm openclaw plugins list
  OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=/tmp/openclaw-proof-state \
  OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=/tmp/openclaw-proof-state/openclaw.json \
    pnpm openclaw plugins inspect memory-core
  ```
- Evidence after fix (copied live CLI output):
  ```text
  Plugins (2/133 enabled)
  │ @openclaw/   │ memory-  │ openclaw │ enabled  │ stock:memory-core/index.js    │ 2026.6.2 │
  │ memory-core  │ core     │          │          │                               │          │
  │ Memory       │ memory-  │ openclaw │ enabled  │ stock:memory-lancedb/index.js │ 2026.6.2 │
  │ LanceDB      │ lancedb  │          │          │                               │          │

  $ openclaw plugins inspect memory-core
  @openclaw/memory-core
  id: memory-core
  Status: loaded
  Origin: bundled
  Commands: dreaming
  ```
- Observed result after fix: With the restrictive allowlist still omitting `memory-core`, the real CLI reports `memory-core` enabled/loaded as the dreaming sidecar (its `dreaming` command is registered) while `memory-lancedb` remains the selected memory slot. Enabled count goes from 1/133 to 2/133, with no other plugin's status changing.
- What was not tested: A live gateway sweep with a real third-party (non-bundled) Dreaming-compatible memory plugin and an LLM credential; `memory-lancedb` (bundled) stood in for the third-party plugin — it exercises the identical activation path (memory slot holder ≠ dreaming engine, dreaming engine omitted from `plugins.allow`).
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: No third-party memory plugin install or LLM API key locally, so the scheduled sweep execution itself was not observed end-to-end. The sweep scheduling depended on the activation layer proven above: the gateway-startup path already includes the dreaming ids unconditionally (`resolveGatewayStartupDreamingPluginIds`, `src/plugins/gateway-startup-plugin-ids.ts`) and only failed because the loader never enabled `memory-core`.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): Same config, same commands on unpatched `main` (8d9ce35):
  ```text
  Plugins (1/133 enabled)
  │ @openclaw/   │ memory-  │ openclaw │ disabled │ stock:memory-core/index.js    │ 2026.6.2 │
  │ memory-core  │ core     │          │          │                               │          │

  $ openclaw plugins inspect memory-core
  Status: disabled
  ```

## Tests and validation

Commands run:
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugins/loader.test.ts` → 160 passed
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugins/channel-plugin-ids.test.ts` → 144 passed
- `pnpm tsgo:core` and `pnpm tsgo:core:test` → no errors
- `pnpm check:import-cycles` → 0 runtime value cycles (config-state now imports memory-host-sdk/dreaming)
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs` on changed files → no findings; `oxfmt` clean; `git diff --check` clean

Regression coverage added: a characterization scenario in `src/plugins/loader.test.ts` ("loads dreaming engine when a restrictive allowlist omits it but dreaming is enabled") that fails on `main` (`expected 'disabled' to be 'loaded'`) and passes with this fix.

What failed before: that scenario, plus the real CLI behavior shown in "Before evidence" above.

Note: `codex review` could not run locally (Codex CLI usage limit); substituted with the typecheck/lint/cycle checks above and the review feedback addressed below.

## Risk checklist

- Did user-visible behavior change? Yes — the dreaming engine now activates (and `openclaw plugins list` reports it enabled) for configs that previously silently skipped it. No change when `memory-core` is denied, disabled via `entries`, dreaming is off, the slot is the default engine, or no restrictive allowlist is set.
- Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? No — no new config keys, defaults, or migrations.
- Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? No.
- What is the highest-risk area? Loosening the allowlist gate for one plugin id. 
- How is that risk mitigated? The exemption requires all of: the id equals the centrally resolved dreaming engine id (only ever bundled `memory-core`, only when dreaming is enabled and a different plugin holds the memory slot), `origin === "bundled"`, and a restrictive allowlist that would otherwise reject it. Denylist, `entries.<id>.enabled: false`, and the workspace default-off gate all return before the exemption.

## Current review state

What is the next action? Maintainer review.

What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof? Nothing on the author. `CI / security-fast` fails on a pre-existing production-dependency advisory (esbuild, GHSA-gv7w-rqvm-qjhr) in the shared lockfile — this PR touches only `src/plugins/{config-activation-shared,config-state,loader,loader.test}.ts` and no dependency files, so that failure is unrelated and maintainer-owned.

Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed? All three Copilot comments: the origin/allowlist scoping (fixed in 455c9253), the comment wording (reworded in 455c9253), and the env-var restore (already handled by the file's global `afterEach` via `resetPluginLoaderTestStateForTest`, see inline reply). ClawSweeper's first review failed on bot-side Codex execution; re-review requested.

target.title: fix(release): tolerate semver build metadata in changelog packaging
target.body:
## Summary

`scripts/package-changelog.mjs` rejects valid semver build metadata, so `pnpm pack` of any package with `version: X.Y.Z+suffix` crashes during prepack with `Unsupported OpenClaw package version for changelog packaging`. pnpm 10 preserves build metadata into the packed manifest, and even on pnpm 11 (which strips it at pack time) the working-directory manifest still carries the suffix through the prepack lifecycle, so the crash hits both. Changelog sections are now resolved from the base version, which is correct because build metadata never changes release identity.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: prepack crash when packing a build with semver build metadata in `package.json`.
Real environment tested: custom-runtime packaging pipeline on Ubuntu (Node 22.22, pnpm 11.2.2), 2026-06-12.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: `pnpm pack` with `version: 2026.6.6+jm`.
Evidence after fix: prepack completes; the packaged CHANGELOG carries the `2026.6.6` release section.
Observed result after fix: tarball builds and installs normally.
What was not tested: nothing material; pure function change covered by unit tests.

## Verification

- Two regression cases added to `test/scripts/package-changelog.test.ts` (`X.Y.Z+meta` and `X.Y.Z-beta.N+meta`); file passes 10/10.

target.title: fix(agents): prevent local exec policy errors from triggering model fallback
target.body:
## Summary

A Codex app-server local exec policy denial was classified as a model failure, so the agent switched providers/models on what is a local configuration outcome - burning fallback budget and changing models mid-session with no availability reason. Policy errors are now excluded from fallback classification. The change also makes an agent-level full exec policy correctly override a global allowlist for the Codex app-server.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: spurious provider/model fallback triggered by local exec policy denials.
Real environment tested: production single-operator OpenClaw gateway, 15 agents, Codex plugin active; patch live since 2026-06-12.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: trigger a denied exec in a Codex app-server lane and observe the session's model routing.
Evidence after fix: denial surfaces as a policy error; fallback chain not activated; session stays on the primary model.
Observed result after fix: policy denials are reported to the operator instead of silently degrading the model.
What was not tested: sibling runtimes with equivalent policy surfaces (non-Codex app-server paths) - flagging for reviewer judgment on whether the same classification gap exists there.

## Verification

- Tests in the same commit: `src/agents/model-fallback.test.ts` (policy errors not treated as fallback failures) and Codex app-server config tests (full exec policy overrides global allowlist).
- Running in production on a patched 2026.6.6 build since 2026-06-12.

target.title: fix(memory-core): preserve keyword-only hybrid results
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes hybrid memory search so keyword-only FTS hits survive the post-merge minScore boundary even when another vector result already passes the strict threshold.

Fixes #92337.

## Real Behavior Proof

- Behavior addressed: Hybrid search no longer drops valid keyword-only FTS hits just because at least one vector result is already strict.
- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout at `/Users/stellarfish/openclaw-92337-boundary`, running the memory-core manager through `node_modules/.bin/tsx --conditions=development` with a real sqlite memory index, trigram FTS enabled, and a local mock embedding provider registered through OpenClaw's provider registry.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: Created an isolated workspace with `memory/vector.md` and `memory/keyword.md`, configured `store.fts.tokenizer: "trigram"`, synced through `getMemorySearchManager(...).sync()`, then ran `manager.search("飞书", { maxResults: 10, minScore: 0.35 })`.
- Evidence after fix:

```json
{
  "query": "飞书",
  "fts": {
    "enabled": true,
    "available": true
  },
  "resultCount": 2,
  "results": [
    {
      "path": "memory/vector.md",
      "score": 0.7,
      "vectorScore": 1,
      "textScore": 0,
      "snippet": "# Vector\nvector-anchor semantic entry\n"
    },
    {
      "path": "memory/keyword.md",
      "score": 0.3,
      "vectorScore": 0,
      "textScore": 1,
      "snippet": "# Keyword\n飞书插件配置 已完成\n"
    }
  ]
}
```

- Observed result after fix: The strict vector result remains first, and the keyword-only trigram FTS result is also preserved with `vectorScore: 0`, `textScore: 1`, and weighted score `0.3`, even though `minScore` is `0.35`.
- What was not tested: Live user Ollama embeddings; the proof uses OpenClaw's real manager/sqlite/FTS path with a deterministic local embedding provider to avoid external credentials.

## Changes

- Move the preservation behavior to the manager search filtering boundary instead of inferring chunk identity from overlapping line ranges.
- Keep `mergeHybridResults` exact-id semantics and leave the shared FTS token normalizer untouched.
- Preserve current main's relaxed keyword-backed fallback when all weighted hybrid scores are below `minScore`, including overlapping vector+keyword hits.
- Preserve keyword-only FTS hits when strict results exist while keeping the order already returned by `mergeHybridResults`, including MMR/temporal ordering.
- Add regression coverage for keyword-only hybrid hits, overlapping keyword-backed fallback, and merge-order preservation.

## Validation

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/memory-core/src/memory/index.test.ts -t "preserves keyword-only hybrid hits|overlapping keyword-backed|hybrid merger order"` — 4 passed, 47 skipped
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/memory-core/src/memory/hybrid.test.ts` — 5 passed
- `./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 extensions/memory-core/src/memory/manager.ts extensions/memory-core/src/memory/index.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.extensions.json extensions/memory-core/src/memory/manager.ts extensions/memory-core/src/memory/index.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.extensions.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/extensions-test.tsbuildinfo`
- `git diff --check -- extensions/memory-core/src/memory/manager.ts extensions/memory-core/src/memory/index.test.ts`

## Risks

- The result window is still capped by `maxResults`, so keyword-only hits can be trimmed if higher-ranked selected results fill the requested limit.

target.title: fix(agents): preserve runtime tool inheritance tokens
target.body:
## Summary
- Preserve runtime-materialized allowlist tokens when spawned sessions inherit an effective parent tool surface.
- Filter deferred MCP/LSP selectors through the same effective profile/agent/group/subagent/inherited policy layers before writing child inherited allowlists, so broad `bundle-mcp`/`group:plugins` entries cannot bypass narrower policies.
- Cover the shared helper, gateway resolution, and embedded tool construction paths, including broad-allow then narrow-allow/deny regressions.

Refs #85030

## Verification
- `corepack pnpm exec oxfmt --check src/agents/tool-policy.ts src/gateway/tool-resolution.ts src/agents/agent-tools.ts src/agents/tool-policy.test.ts src/gateway/tool-resolution.exclude.test.ts src/agents/agent-tools.create-openclaw-coding-tools.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/tool-policy.test.ts src/gateway/tool-resolution.exclude.test.ts src/agents/agent-tools.create-openclaw-coding-tools.test.ts`
- `corepack pnpm test:docker:cron-mcp-cleanup`
- Temporary local proof test, removed after capture: `corepack pnpm exec vitest run src/agents/openclaw-sessions-spawn-mcp-proof.temp.test.ts --reporter verbose`

## Real behavior proof
Behavior addressed: Spawned subagent inherited allowlists dropped runtime materialization policy tokens such as `bundle-mcp`, `group:plugins`, MCP `server__tool` names, and `lsp_` tool names when the parent effective tool surface did not already contain materialized runtime tools; preserving those tokens must also respect narrower effective policy layers.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw source checkout on macOS, Docker 29.4.0, repository Docker E2E image built from this branch, real Gateway process, mock OpenAI-compatible server, and real stdio MCP probe servers. The focused spawned-child MCP proof used the real `sessions_spawn` tool executor, the production subagent spawn executor, production child session patching, production `getOrCreateSessionMcpRuntime`/`materializeBundleMcpToolsForRun`, child attempt allowlist filtering, and final effective tool policy.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: After commit `419086e4c141d03874d7bec1730c099a96a84b55`, ran `corepack pnpm test:docker:cron-mcp-cleanup`. Then ran a temporary focused proof test with:

```text
corepack pnpm exec vitest run src/agents/openclaw-sessions-spawn-mcp-proof.temp.test.ts --reporter verbose
```

That proof created a temporary stdio MCP server with two tools, `allowed` and `blocked`; configured `sessions_spawn` with the narrowed inherited allowlist `["read", "sessions_spawn", "probe__allowed"]`; executed a real `sessions_spawn` call; captured the child session patch written by the spawn executor; wrote the captured child store to the configured session store path; materialized both MCP tools for the spawned child session; then ran the same child attempt allowlist/final policy filtering and invoked the surviving MCP tool.

Evidence after fix: Focused spawned-child terminal output with UUIDs redacted:

```json
{
  "sessionsSpawnInheritedAllowInput": [
    "read",
    "sessions_spawn",
    "probe__allowed"
  ],
  "spawnResult": {
    "status": "accepted",
    "childSessionKey": "agent:main:subagent:<uuid>",
    "runId": "proof-run-1",
    "mode": "run",
    "modelApplied": true
  },
  "childSessionKey": "agent:main:subagent:<uuid>",
  "childInheritedAllow": [
    "read",
    "sessions_spawn",
    "probe__allowed"
  ],
  "capturedStoreEntry": {
    "spawnDepth": 1,
    "subagentRole": "orchestrator",
    "subagentControlScope": "children",
    "inheritedToolAllow": [
      "read",
      "sessions_spawn",
      "probe__allowed"
    ],
    "spawnedBy": "agent:main:main"
  },
  "gatewayAgentCallSeen": true,
  "materializedToolNames": [
    "probe__allowed",
    "probe__blocked"
  ],
  "childVisibleMcpToolNames": [
    "probe__allowed"
  ],
  "blockedVisible": false,
  "invocationResult": {
    "content": [
      {
        "type": "text",
        "text": "subagent-sessions-spawn-mcp-allowed-ok"
      }
    ],
    "details": {
      "mcpServer": "probe",
      "mcpTool": "allowed"
    }
  }
}
```

The temporary proof passed in both Vitest projects that selected it:

```text
✓ |agents-core| proof: real sessions_spawn child receives and enforces narrowed MCP inherited allowlist
✓ |agents-support| proof: real sessions_spawn child receives and enforces narrowed MCP inherited allowlist

Test Files  2 passed (2)
Tests       2 passed (2)
```

Additional real Docker output from `corepack pnpm test:docker:cron-mcp-cleanup`:

```text
OpenClaw package tarball integrity passed.
Running in-container cron/subagent MCP cleanup smoke...
{"ok":true,"cron":{"jobId":"ed353e39-08bc-408e-8867-6a99df36a679","runId":"manual:ed353e39-08bc-408e-8867-6a99df36a679:1781294149608:1","pid":282,"status":"ok"},"subagent":{"runId":"d6a7af12-ed53-416a-b917-8f0c234d2f14","exitedPids":[295],"pids":[295]}}
OK
```

Observed result after fix: The real `sessions_spawn` path accepted a child run, wrote `inheritedToolAllow: ["read", "sessions_spawn", "probe__allowed"]` onto the spawned child session, the child materialized both MCP tools from the stdio server, final child-visible tools included only `probe__allowed`, the conflicting `probe__blocked` tool was absent, and the allowed MCP tool executed successfully.

What was not tested: A credentialed external MCP server or a live paid LLM provider. The proof uses local stdio MCP servers and the repository mock/test runtime for deterministic behavior.

target.title: Fix Codex synthetic usage in status without local OpenAI profiles
target.body:
## Summary
- route `/status` Codex synthetic usage through the Codex app-server marker even when no local OpenAI OAuth/token profile label exists
- teach shared `status --usage` to add the Codex synthetic OpenAI usage snapshot only for configured OpenAI Codex runtime routes whose effective auth label is not API-key-backed
- keep API-key/OpenClaw-pinned status contexts from probing Codex synthetic usage, with regression coverage for no-profile, API-key-backed, and OpenClaw-pinned cases

Closes #92506

## Linked context
- Issue: #92506
- ClawSweeper intake: source-repro, queueable-fix, fix-shape-clear
- Current PR head: `08e19d72ac`
- Upstream contract checked locally in sibling `../codex`: `account/rateLimits/read` maps to `GetAccountRateLimits` and returns Codex rate-limit snapshots used by the existing Codex provider usage hook

## Real behavior proof
- Behavior or issue addressed: `status --usage` should show real Codex/OpenAI usage windows when the default OpenAI model is effectively running on the Codex runtime, even if the status process does not have a local OpenAI OAuth/token profile label. Current 2026.6.6 omitted the Codex/OpenAI usage line.
- Real environment tested: production OpenClaw VPS `polymarket-mc`, Ubuntu arm64, prod OpenClaw home/config/gateway, gateway still running from installed OpenClaw 2026.6.6. After-fix command was run from an isolated PR checkout at `~/oc-proof-92520`, commit `220380b3`, without replacing or restarting the prod gateway service.
- Current-head delta after proof: `08e19d72ac` only adds the shared CLI effective-auth guard so API-key-backed OpenAI Codex runtime routes do not receive the synthetic app-server quota query; it does not change the proven no-profile app-server path.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```text
# before, installed production CLI
openclaw --version
openclaw status --usage

# after, isolated PR checkout against the same real ~/.openclaw state and gateway
cd ~/oc-proof-92520
node openclaw.mjs --version
node openclaw.mjs status --usage
```

- Evidence after fix:

```text
=== BEFORE installed openclaw version ===
OpenClaw 2026.6.6 (8c802aa)
=== BEFORE installed openclaw status --usage ===
Sessions
... default gpt-5.5 ... Runtime OpenAI Codex ...

Usage
Usage:
  Claude: HTTP 403: OAuth token <redacted> not meet scope requirement user:profile
```

```text
=== AFTER PR checkout openclaw version ===
OpenClaw 2026.6.2 (220380b)
=== AFTER PR checkout openclaw status --usage ===
Overview
  Git                  fix/92506-codex-synthetic-usage-status · @ 220380b3
  Gateway              local · ws://127.0.0.1:18789 ... app 2026.6.6 linux 6.17.0-1011-oracle
  Sessions             255 active · default gpt-5.5 (200k ctx) · 2 stores

Sessions
... default gpt-5.5 ... Runtime OpenAI Codex ...

Usage
Usage:
  Claude: HTTP 403: OAuth token <redacted> not meet scope requirement user:profile
  OpenAI (pro (<redacted> credits))
    5h: 86% left · resets 58m
    Week: 86% left · resets 5d 4h
```

- Observed result after fix: the same VPS/prod config that previously rendered only the Claude usage error now renders real Codex/OpenAI usage from the app-server route, including both `5h` and `Week` windows. The gateway stayed on the installed prod 2026.6.6 service; only the CLI process came from the isolated PR checkout.
- What was not tested: I did not post Telegram `/status` screenshots because the real CLI `status --usage` output exercises the requested status usage path and ClawSweeper accepted copied live output as sufficient. The after command did not replace or restart the production gateway.

## Supplemental tests and validation
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/status-runtime-shared.test.ts -- --reporter=verbose --pool=forks --maxWorkers=1 --fileParallelism=false`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/commands-status.test.ts src/commands/status-runtime-shared.test.ts src/commands/status-json.test.ts src/infra/provider-usage.auth.plugin.test.ts src/infra/provider-usage.load.plugin.test.ts -- --reporter=verbose --pool=forks --maxWorkers=1 --fileParallelism=false`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/codex/provider.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/rate-limits.test.ts -- --reporter=verbose --pool=forks --maxWorkers=1 --fileParallelism=false`
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json --noEmit --incremental false`
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.extensions.json --noEmit --incremental false`
- `git diff --check`
- `codex -c service_tier='''fast''' review --base origin/main` -> no actionable regressions found before the current-head API-key guard follow-up

## Risk checklist
- No merge, land, or automerge performed
- No changelog added; scoped status/runtime bugfix with regression tests
- Current-head CI has one unrelated cleanup-flake failure in `checks-node-agentic-control-plane-agent-chat`: `ENOTEMPTY` while removing `/tmp/openclaw-gw-*`; GitHub permissions did not allow me to rerun it
- ClawSweeper re-review requested after adding real VPS proof and the API-key guard follow-up

target.title: Suppress failed tool progress in Discord
target.body:
## Summary

- skip failed/error item progress before Discord draft rendering
- skip failed command-output progress before Discord draft rendering
- preserve existing fallback tool-error warning delivery for unacknowledged failures
- add Discord monitor regressions for failed item progress and failed command output

## Verification

- `pnpm test extensions/discord/src/monitor/message-handler.process.test.ts` passes: 106 tests
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 extensions/discord/src/monitor/message-handler.process.ts extensions/discord/src/monitor/message-handler.process.test.ts`
- `pnpm exec oxlint --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.extensions.json extensions/discord/src/monitor/message-handler.process.ts extensions/discord/src/monitor/message-handler.process.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Discord can leak internal failed tool progress as channel-visible draft text after an otherwise normal run.

Real environment tested: Local Vitest regression coverage in a fresh fork checkout; separately observed a locally patched installed Discord canary suppressing the failed progress leak before preparing this PR.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `pnpm test extensions/discord/src/monitor/message-handler.process.test.ts`.

Evidence after fix: The Discord monitor test file passes all 106 tests, including new assertions that failed item progress and failed command output do not update Discord draft text.

Observed result after fix: Failed/error tool progress remains out of Discord-visible draft output while existing fallback tool-error warning behavior is preserved by leaving `suppressToolErrorWarnings` unset.

What was not tested: No live Discord bot run was executed from this source checkout, and broader extension/full CI was not run locally.

target.title: Add Telegram voice STT telemetry and cron payload audit
target.body:
## Summary
- add Telegram voice/STT handoff telemetry into inbound context, including download bytes/MIME/path, Telegram file metadata, STT provider/model/base URL, duration, error class, transcript length/hash/trust/truncation, and context-entry marker
- label missing, timed-out, truncated, disabled, and untrusted transcripts instead of silently treating bad or absent STT text as the spoken message
- add computed cron payload audit metadata for deterministic `command` jobs vs script-shaped legacy `agentTurn` jobs

Closes #92500
Closes #92501
Parent: electricsheephq/evaos-support-control#161

## Real behavior proof
- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Scheduled jobs that hide deterministic shell/script work inside `agentTurn` payloads are now detected and surfaced as audit warnings, while explicit `command` jobs are identified as deterministic. This is the OpenClaw-side proof for replacing hidden Matt/Malory agentTurn scripts with manifest-visible deterministic jobs.
- **Real environment tested:** Patched OpenClaw worktree on the local Codex host at `/Volumes/LEXAR/repos/worktrees/openclaw-matt-malory-stt-scheduler`, executing the changed TypeScript runtime module with `tsx` against the real source files on this branch.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** `npx --yes tsx --no-cache --eval ...` imported `./src/cron/payload-audit.ts`, evaluated a script-shaped legacy `agentTurn`, and evaluated an explicit deterministic `command` payload.
- **Evidence after fix:** Terminal output from the patched runtime command:

```console
$ npx --yes tsx --no-cache --eval 'import { resolveCronPayloadAudit } from "./src/cron/payload-audit.ts"; const hidden = resolveCronPayloadAudit({ kind: "agentTurn", message: "Run this exact scheduled job.\n```bash\nnode scripts/matt-malory-watchdog.js --json\n```" }); const command = resolveCronPayloadAudit({ kind: "command", argv: ["openclaw", "support", "sync", "--json"] }); console.log(JSON.stringify({ hidden, command }, null, 2));'
{
  "hidden": {
    "executionKind": "agent-turn",
    "deterministic": false,
    "warnings": [
      {
        "code": "hidden-agent-turn-script",
        "severity": "warn",
        "message": "This scheduled agentTurn appears to hide a deterministic script. Prefer payload.kind=\"command\" with an explicit argv vector for scheduled command jobs.",
        "recommendation": "Convert the job payload to { \"kind\": \"command\", \"argv\": [...] } so scheduler JSON exposes the deterministic command directly."
      }
    ]
  },
  "command": {
    "executionKind": "deterministic-command",
    "deterministic": true,
    "warnings": []
  }
}
```

- **Observed result after fix:** The legacy script-shaped `agentTurn` returned `executionKind: "agent-turn"`, `deterministic: false`, and a `hidden-agent-turn-script` warning. The explicit `command` payload returned `executionKind: "deterministic-command"`, `deterministic: true`, and no warnings.
- **What was not tested:** A live Matt Telegram OGG message and a live Matt VM scheduled-job migration were not triggered from this PR. Those remain Sprint 4 canary acceptance after the platform code is deployed.

## Local validation
- `git diff --check`
- `node --check scripts/run-vitest.mjs`
- real patched-code smoke shown above with `npx --yes tsx --no-cache --eval ...`

## Deferred to GitHub CI
- Dependency-hydrated Vitest/protocol/architecture validation is intentionally delegated to GitHub CI for this large OpenClaw workspace. The clean local worktree does not keep `node_modules` hydrated.

target.title: fix(opencode): restore Zen model catalog
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: the bundled `opencode` Zen provider lacked the provider-owned catalog/resolver that its sibling `opencode-go` already had, so users had to hand-register Zen models and routes; review also showed `minimax-m2.7` was being sent to the wrong Zen transport.
- Solution: restore the Zen provider catalog, dynamic resolver, catalog augmentation, route normalization, and manifest metadata at the `opencode` plugin boundary.
- What changed: the PR adds the Zen static seed, runtime discovery, family-specific routing, and tests; this update filters live discovery to provider-owned metadata rows only and routes MiniMax through the documented Zen OpenAI-compatible chat completions endpoint.
- What did NOT change: this follow-up does not change non-OpenCode providers, `opencode-go` runtime behavior, generic LLM adapter APIs, or the shared OpenCode credential fallback policy.
- Why it matters / User impact: users can select advertised `opencode/<model>` Zen models without hand-writing every model row, while ID-only live discovery can no longer make unknown rows selectable with guessed metadata and MiniMax no longer hits the failing Anthropic Messages path.
- Fixes #92479

## Origin / follow-up

- **Follows / completes:** This updates the existing #92495 repair branch after ClawSweeper flagged live-only Zen row synthesis and missing successful MiniMax live proof in the same PR.
- **Gap this fills:** The previous PR head restored the catalog but still synthesized full metadata for live IDs missing from the static seed, and grouped MiniMax with the Zen Anthropic Messages route even though live Zen completion succeeds on the documented OpenAI-compatible chat completions route.
- **Consistency:** The live-discovery behavior matches the shared `buildLiveModelProviderConfig` pattern used by `opencode-go`: filter to provider-owned metadata rows and fall back to the static seed when discovery is unavailable or yields no owned rows. The MiniMax route now matches the documented Zen `/v1/chat/completions` behavior proven with a redacted Zen key.

## Competition / linked PR analysis

- **Linked PR(s):** #92495 is the existing linked PR for #92479; `check-upstream-fixed` found `origin/main` does not already cover this repair.
- **Related open PR / same-contract scan:** The linked issue and ClawSweeper review identify this PR as the active candidate fix. I did not find a same-contract canonical PR on `origin/main` superseding the Zen catalog owner-boundary repair.
- **Gap in linked PR(s):** The current linked PR itself had two author-actionable code gaps: live-only discovery rows were exposed with inferred route/input/pricing/context/max-token metadata, and MiniMax was routed through the Zen Anthropic Messages endpoint that returned 401 for the provided valid Zen key.
- **Why this patch is better/canonical:** It fixes both gaps at the provider-owned catalog/routing boundary instead of adding downstream special cases.
- **Local real behavior proof advantage:** The focused provider test exercises live discovery with a known row plus unknown row, and an unknown-only discovery response, so the metadata ownership boundary is locked in local code proof. A redacted live request now proves the current `minimax-m2.7` route returns successful model output.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** The bundled `opencode` Zen provider exposed auth/setup but did not expose a model catalog or dynamic model resolver, so `opencode/<model>` selections failed as unknown or unavailable. This patch restores the provider-owned Zen catalog, keeps a static seed visible when live discovery is unavailable or has no owned rows, filters live-only IDs until metadata is provider-owned, resolves shared OpenCode credentials for the Zen catalog, and routes Zen model families through the expected OpenClaw adapters. The follow-up also fixes `minimax-m2.7` so the current route is `openai-completions` at `https://opencode.ai/zen/v1`, which maps to the documented `https://opencode.ai/zen/v1/chat/completions` endpoint.
- **Real environment tested:** Linux, Node 22.22.0, pnpm 11.2.2, local OpenClaw PR worktree. The MiniMax live proof used a redacted OpenCode Zen API key only as a transient environment variable; the raw key is not printed, stored, or included here.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

```bash
source "$EVIDENCE_DIR/context.env"
cd "$TASK_WORKTREE"

NO_COLOR=1 CI=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs \
  extensions/opencode/index.test.ts \
  > "$EVIDENCE_DIR/opencode-index-minimax-route-red-20260619.log" 2>&1

NO_COLOR=1 CI=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs \
  extensions/opencode/index.test.ts \
  > "$EVIDENCE_DIR/opencode-index-minimax-route-green-20260619.log" 2>&1

OPENCODE_ZEN_API_KEY="[REDACTED]" curl -4 \
  -H 'content-type: application/json' \
  -H 'authorization: Bearer [REDACTED]' \
  --data '{"model":"minimax-m2.7","max_tokens":128,"temperature":0,"messages":[{"role":"system","content":"Return only the requested sentinel."},{"role":"user","content":"Reply exactly with OPENCLAW_MINIMAX_ROUTE_OK."}]}' \
  https://opencode.ai/zen/v1/chat/completions
```

- **Evidence after fix:** The RED test first proved the previous route was wrong for `minimax-m2.7`:

```text
FAIL  |extension-misc| extensions/opencode/index.test.ts > opencode provider plugin > keeps OpenCode Zen catalog coverage aligned with the curated seed
AssertionError: expected { id: 'minimax-m2.7', …(10) } to match object { api: 'openai-completions', …(1) }

- Expected
+ Received

  {
-   "api": "openai-completions",
-   "baseUrl": "https://opencode.ai/zen/v1",
+   "api": "anthropic-messages",
+   "baseUrl": "https://opencode.ai/zen",
  }

Test Files  1 failed (1)
Tests  1 failed | 9 passed (10)
red-test-exit-status=1
```

The GREEN test then passed after the provider resolver and manifest rows were updated:

```text
Test Files  1 passed (1)
     Tests  10 passed (10)
  Duration  27.22s (transform 17.30s, setup 753ms, import 25.48s, tests 568ms, environment 0ms)

green-test-exit-status=0
```

The current route was then exercised against Zen with the key redacted. The manifest route matches the documented endpoint and returns successful `minimax-m2.7` model output:

```text
manifest_model=minimax-m2.7
manifest_api=openai-completions
manifest_baseUrl=https://opencode.ai/zen/v1
current_route_endpoint=https://opencode.ai/zen/v1/chat/completions
documented_endpoint=https://opencode.ai/zen/v1/chat/completions
current_route_matches_documented=true
request_auth=Authorization: Bearer [REDACTED]
curl_exit_status=0
response_status=200
response_ok=true
response_type=chat.completion
response_model=accounts/fireworks/models/minimax-m2p7
response_finish_reason=stop
response_content_preview=OPENCLAW_MINIMAX_ROUTE_OK
response_error=null
secret-scan=pass
current-route-live-proof-exit-status=0
```

Previous authenticated proof before this MiniMax route repair covered discovery and representative live completions through Claude/Anthropic Messages, GPT/OpenAI Responses, Gemini/Google Generative AI, Qwen/Anthropic Messages, and MiniMax M2.5/OpenAI-compatible paths.

```text
authenticated_discovery_models=48
authenticated_discovery_request url=https://opencode.ai/zen/v1/models authorization_header=present status=200
shared_opencode_go_credential_catalog_models=48
live_request family=Claude model=claude-haiku-4-5 api=anthropic-messages baseUrl=https://opencode.ai/zen http_status=200 stopReason=stop text="ok"
live_request family=GPT model=gpt-5-nano api=openai-responses baseUrl=https://opencode.ai/zen/v1 http_status=200 stopReason=stop text="ok"
live_request family=Gemini model=gemini-3-flash api=google-generative-ai baseUrl=https://opencode.ai/zen/v1 stopReason=stop text="ok"
live_request family=Qwen model=qwen3.6-plus api=anthropic-messages baseUrl=https://opencode.ai/zen http_status=200 stopReason=stop text="ok"
live_request family=MiniMax model=minimax-m2.5 api=openai-completions baseUrl=https://opencode.ai/zen/v1 http_status=200 stopReason=stop text="ok"
result=passed
```

- **Observed result after fix:** `opencode` now exposes the Zen catalog, `minimax-m2.7` is no longer routed through the failed Zen Anthropic Messages path, the current MiniMax route matches the documented OpenAI-compatible chat completions endpoint, and live discovery no longer makes ID-only unknown Zen rows selectable with guessed metadata.
- **What was not tested:** I did not change or prove the provider-owner policy decisions for the full 48-row static seed or the shared `opencode`/`opencode-go` credential fallback. Those remain intentionally disclosed maintainer/provider-owner review items.

## Review findings addressed

- **RF-001 — successful redacted `minimax-m2.7` live output:** Fixed. The current manifest route is `api=openai-completions`, `baseUrl=https://opencode.ai/zen/v1`, and the redacted live request returned `response_status=200`, `response_ok=true`, and `response_content_preview=OPENCLAW_MINIMAX_ROUTE_OK`.
- **RF-002 — stronger real behavior proof before merge:** Fixed for the author-actionable MiniMax route proof. The RED test caught the old route, the GREEN test passed after the minimal route repair, and the redacted live request exercised the current route against Zen successfully.
- **RF-003 — 48-row static seed drift risk:** Disclosed as provider-owner policy risk. The static seed remains because the linked issue is a regression from catalog-backed Zen to hand registration, and it is the only offline source of rich metadata when discovery is unavailable. Maintainers may still choose a smaller seed before merge.
- **RF-004 — shared `opencode`/`opencode-go` credential fallback:** Disclosed as provider-owner policy risk. The fallback mirrors the sibling OpenCode Go catalog path and stays bounded to OpenCode catalog loading, but overlaps the open split-key policy discussion.
- **RF-005 — latest after-fix MiniMax proof returned 401:** Fixed. The latest current-route live proof now uses `https://opencode.ai/zen/v1/chat/completions` with redacted Bearer auth and returns HTTP 200 plus successful model output.
- **RF-006 — next action is provider-owner review plus successful MiniMax proof:** Author-actionable proof is now supplied. Remaining provider-owner review is limited to accepting the 48-row seed and shared credential policy boundaries disclosed above.
- **Live-only Zen row synthesis:** Fixed in `extensions/opencode/provider-catalog.ts` and locked by `extensions/opencode/index.test.ts`; `buildDiscoveredOpencodeZenModels` now only returns model rows already present in the provider-owned static seed.

## Regression Test Plan

- **Target test file:** `extensions/opencode/index.test.ts` plus redacted live evidence stored in the evidence directory outside the repo worktree.
- **Scenario locked in:** Regression tests assert the Zen catalog exposes the 48 static model rows, dynamic model resolution works for Zen IDs, shared `opencode-go` credentials populate the `opencode` catalog path, Claude/GPT/Gemini/Qwen/MiniMax families route to the expected adapter/base URL pairs, live-only discovery rows are filtered until metadata is owned, and static fallback remains visible when live discovery is unavailable or returns no owned IDs.
- **Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail:** A focused provider test locks the source-of-truth catalog/routing contract without adding broad adapter mocks, while the live proof covers the external provider boundary for the exact MiniMax route ClawSweeper requested.

## Merge risk

- **Risk labels considered:** `merge-risk: 🚨 compatibility`, `merge-risk: 🚨 auth-provider`, provider catalog/routing, generated manifest metadata, and large-diff catalog seed review cost.
- **Risk explanation:** The compatibility risk is that a provider-owned seed can drift from Zen if the upstream catalog changes before live discovery runs. The auth-provider risk is bounded to resolving the same OpenCode credential across `opencode` and `opencode-go`; no key is stored in the repo or PR body. The large diff is mostly generated manifest/catalog rows rather than a cross-cutting runtime refactor. The MiniMax proof risk is now reduced by the redacted successful `minimax-m2.7` live completion on the current route; provider-owner review is still needed for the seed/auth policy boundaries.
- **Why acceptable:** The previous behavior left Zen effectively unusable without hand registration. The patch restores the provider contract at its owner boundary, keeps live discovery as the preferred source when credentials are present, prevents live-only ID metadata synthesis, preserves a no-discovery local seed, and fixes the concrete MiniMax routing defect surfaced by review.

## Root Cause

- **Root cause:** The `opencode` Zen provider was migrated as an auth/setup surface without owning the Zen model catalog contract that `opencode-go` already supported. Zen live discovery also returns ID-only rows, so unowned IDs cannot safely become selectable rich model rows without provider-owned metadata. Separately, MiniMax was grouped with Claude/Qwen on the Zen Anthropic Messages route even though live Zen behavior proves MiniMax runs through the documented OpenAI-compatible chat completions route.
- **Why this is root-cause fix:** The patch restores the missing provider-owned catalog/resolver boundary, keeps rich metadata owned by the provider seed, and fixes MiniMax route selection before downstream adapter execution instead of adding downstream string special cases or guessing metadata from ID-only discovery.
- **Fix classification:** Root-cause provider catalog and routing repair for the bundled `opencode` Zen provider.
- **Maintainer-ready confidence:** High for the author-actionable source repair: the catalog regression, ClawSweeper live-only synthesis finding, and successful MiniMax live proof request are now covered by fresh provider tests and redacted live evidence.
- **Patch quality notes:** The fallback/default warnings are expected catalog-boundary behavior, not symptom masking: live model catalogs are advisory, and provider-owned static rows remain visible when discovery is unavailable or returns no owned metadata rows. The large diff is mostly static/generated catalog metadata needed to restore the regressed Zen catalog. Out of scope: changing non-OpenCode providers, generic adapter APIs, the sibling `opencode-go` runtime, the full 48-row seed policy, or the shared OpenCode credential fallback policy.
- **Architecture / source-of-truth check:** The provider plugin is the source-of-truth owner for bundled provider catalog metadata. This changes catalog/routing before downstream adapter selection rather than adding mirrored/persisted records or generic runtime fallbacks.

target.title: fix(mcp): reject blocked stdio env keys at write time and warn once per server
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #92474.

`docs/cli/mcp.md` ("Stdio env safety filter") promises that interpreter-startup env keys such as `PYTHONPATH` and `NODE_OPTIONS` in a stdio MCP server's `env` block are **rejected with a configuration error**. The implementation instead saved them silently and then logged

```
bundle-mcp: server "my_server": env "PYTHONPATH" is blocked for stdio startup safety and was ignored.
```

on **every** transport resolution — `mcp status`, `mcp probe`, and each spawn — flooding the gateway journal for frequently invoked servers.

This PR aligns the implementation with the documented contract. All boundaries reuse the same policy predicate (`isDangerousMcpStdioEnvVarName`), so write, validation, and runtime behavior cannot disagree:

1. **Write boundary** — `setConfiguredMcpServer` / `updateConfiguredMcpServer` now return a clear error naming the blocked keys *before* writing, so `mcp set`, `mcp add`, and `mcp configure` fail at the command.
2. **Validation boundary** — `McpServerSchema` gains a `superRefine` that reports each blocked env key at its exact path, so `config validate`, doctor, and startup surface the offending key.
3. **Runtime** — the resolver still drops blocked keys from configs written by hand or by older builds (defense in depth), but now warns **once per server+key per process** instead of on every resolution. A test hook (`resetDroppedMcpEnvWarningsForTests`) keeps the existing warning tests deterministic.
4. **Doctor recovery** *(added per ClawSweeper review)* — a focused legacy-config migration (`mcp.servers.env.blocked-stdio-keys` in `legacy-config-migrations.runtime.mcp.ts`, alongside the existing MCP `type→transport` migration) removes blocked env keys from **command-bearing servers only** during `openclaw doctor --fix`, reporting each removal as an operator-visible change. Benign env keys and HTTP/SSE servers are preserved. This makes the validation error's existing `doctor --fix` guidance true, so configs saved by released v2026.6.6 builds recover zero-touch.

The refinement only applies to command-bearing (stdio) servers; HTTP/SSE servers and ordinary credential/proxy env vars (`GITHUB_TOKEN`, `HTTP_PROXY`, `PATH`, custom `*_API_KEY`) are unaffected — the existing `mcp set … env.PATH` doctor tests still pass unchanged.

## Real behavior proof

Captured against the CLI built from this branch (`pnpm build`, commit 4ef4912) on Ubuntu 24.04 / Node v22.22.0, in a throwaway `$HOME` (temp paths left unredacted — nothing sensitive).

**1. Blocked save rejection — `mcp set` with `PYTHONPATH` exits 1, nothing written:**

```console
$ openclaw mcp set pytools '{"command":"/usr/bin/python3","args":["server.py"],"env":{"PYTHONPATH":"/srv/workspace","PYTHONUNBUFFERED":"1"}}'
MCP env "PYTHONPATH" is blocked for stdio startup safety. Remove it from the server env block or set it on the gateway host process instead.
(exit code: 1)
```

**2. Benign save success — same server without the blocked key saves normally:**

```console
$ openclaw mcp set pytools '{"command":"/usr/bin/python3","args":["server.py"],"env":{"PYTHONUNBUFFERED":"1"}}'
Saved MCP server "pytools" to /tmp/tmp.pprYfowE0P/.openclaw/openclaw.json.
(exit code: 0)
```

**3. Exact-path configuration validation — a config carrying `PYTHONPATH` (as saved by an older current-main build) fails `config validate` with the precise key path:**

```console
$ cat ~/.openclaw/openclaw.json
{
  "mcp": {
    "servers": {
      "pytools": {
        "command": "/usr/bin/python3",
        "args": ["server.py"],
        "env": { "PYTHONPATH": "/srv/workspace", "PYTHONUNBUFFERED": "1" }
      }
    }
  }
}

$ openclaw config validate
OpenClaw config is invalid: ~/.openclaw/openclaw.json
  × mcp.servers.pytools.env.PYTHONPATH: env "PYTHONPATH" is blocked for stdio startup safety; set it on the gateway host process instead of the MCP server env block

Run `openclaw doctor --fix` to repair, or fix the keys above manually.
Inspect with openclaw config validate.
(exit code: 1)
```

**4. Doctor recovery — `doctor --fix` repairs a released-build config carrying `PYTHONPATH`, and the repaired config passes validation:**

```console
$ openclaw doctor --fix --non-interactive   # same config as proof 3 (excerpts)
◇  Legacy config keys detected ──────────────────────────────────────────╮
│  - mcp.servers: mcp.servers stdio env blocks contain keys blocked for  │
│    startup safety; they are ignored at launch and fail config          │
│    validation. Run "openclaw doctor --fix".                            │
◇  Doctor changes ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╮
│  Removed mcp.servers.pytools.env.PYTHONPATH (blocked for stdio startup  │
│  safety; set it on the gateway host process instead).                   │

$ cat ~/.openclaw/openclaw.json   # after doctor --fix (mcp section)
  "mcp": {
    "servers": {
      "pytools": {
        "command": "/usr/bin/python3",
        "args": ["server.py"],
        "env": { "PYTHONUNBUFFERED": "1" }
      }
    }
  }

$ openclaw config validate
Config valid: ~/.openclaw/openclaw.json
(exit code: 0)
```

(The doctor run also printed an unrelated probe warning because the dummy `/usr/bin/python3 server.py` MCP server doesn't actually exist in the throwaway environment — expected, and independent of the migration.)

Warning deduplication is covered by the new unit test in `src/agents/mcp-transport-config.test.ts` ("warns once per server and env key across repeated resolutions"): three resolutions of the same server produce exactly one warning; a differently named server warns again.

## Tests

- `src/config/mcp-config.test.ts`: blocked key fails `setConfiguredMcpServer` without writing; benign interpreter keys (`PYTHONUNBUFFERED`) still save.
- `src/cli/mcp-cli.test.ts`: `mcp set` exits 1 with the new error and does not persist the server.
- `src/agents/mcp-transport-config.test.ts`: repeated resolutions of the same server warn once; a different server name warns again.
- `src/commands/doctor/shared/legacy-config-migrate.test.ts`: migration removes blocked keys from command-bearing servers and preserves benign keys; non-stdio servers and benign-only env blocks are untouched (no spurious changes).
- `src/commands/doctor/shared/legacy-config-migrate.validation.test.ts`: post-repair proof — a released-build config with `PYTHONPATH` migrates to a config that passes full schema validation (`partiallyValid` undefined), with `PYTHONUNBUFFERED` and the HTTP server preserved.

## Verification

All commands from the review's acceptance criteria:

```
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/doctor-config-flow.test.ts src/commands/doctor-legacy-config.migrations.test.ts src/config/mcp-config.test.ts src/cli/mcp-cli.test.ts src/agents/mcp-transport-config.test.ts   # 4 shards, 132 tests passed
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/doctor/shared/legacy-config-migrate.test.ts src/commands/doctor/shared/legacy-config-migrate.validation.test.ts   # 122 tests passed
pnpm tsgo:core            # clean
pnpm tsgo:test:src        # clean
pnpm check:base-config-schema   # no drift
git diff --check          # clean
oxfmt --check / oxlint on touched files   # clean
```

## Compatibility / recovery policy

The review established that v2026.6.6 (commit 8c802aa) shipped the save-then-warn write path, so released users can hold configs this PR newly invalidates. This revision resolves that with the recommended **focused doctor repair**: `openclaw doctor --fix` now removes blocked stdio env keys from command-bearing MCP servers with an operator-visible notice (proof item 4), making the validation error's existing `doctor --fix` guidance accurate. Recovery is zero-touch; manual editing remains possible and the validation error names the exact key path either way.

## Docs

`docs/cli/mcp.md` Stdio env safety filter warning updated to mention that `mcp set` / `mcp add` reject the keys at save time and that `config validate` reports the exact key path.

The branch adds task-registry maintenance reconciliation for stale running subagent tasks by matching a terminal scoped CLI child with the same runId and ownerKey, propagating terminal status, and adding regression tests.

PR surface: Source +62, Tests +150. Total +212 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main keeps childless non-Codex subagent tasks backed, so maintenance does not reconcile the reported parent from a terminal CLI sibling. I did not reproduce the intermittent live process-loss trigger.

target.title: fix(tasks): reconcile stale subagent tasks when backing CLI child is terminal (fixes #92285)
target.body:
### Summary

- **Problem**: When a backing CLI child task becomes `lost`, the parent subagent task (runtime: `subagent`, no `childSessionKey`) remains `running`, and the linked `task_mirrored` TaskFlow also stays `running` — even after gateway restart. Task maintenance flags the parent as `stale_running` in diagnostics but never auto-reconciles it.
- **Root Cause**: `hasBackingSession()` falls through to `!isChildlessCodexNativeSubagentTask(task)` when a subagent task has no `childSessionKey`. Since `isChildlessCodexNativeSubagentTask` only matches `runtime: "codex-native"`, the guard unconditionally returns `true` for `runtime: "subagent"`. This prevents `shouldMarkLost` from ever reconciling the stale parent task.
- **Fix**: Pre-compute a `Set<string>` of terminal `runId`s during the maintenance sweep, then add a reconciliation block after the existing `shouldMarkLost` path that marks subagent tasks as `lost` when a terminal task sharing the same `runId` exists. The downstream `markTaskLost` → `updateTask` → `syncFlowFromTaskAfterTaskMutation` call chain automatically terminalizes the linked `task_mirrored` TaskFlow.
- **What changed**:
  - `src/tasks/task-registry.maintenance.ts` — add terminal-runId pre-computation (6 lines) and subagent reconciliation block (25 lines) inside `runTaskRegistryMaintenance()`
  - `src/tasks/task-registry.maintenance.issue-60299.test.ts` — add regression test for the stale-subagent-with-terminal-child scenario (36 lines)
- **What did NOT change (scope boundary)**:
  - `hasBackingSession()` — intentionally left unchanged to avoid broadening the scope of an existing predicate that other callers rely on
  - TaskFlow maintenance (`task-flow-registry.maintenance.ts`) — not touched; the existing `syncFlowFromTaskAfterTaskMutation` hook inside `updateTask` already handles flow terminalization when task status changes
  - Live task propagation — not touched; only the maintenance sweep path is addressed

### Reproduction

1. Start a full-agent child task through an orchestrator using `sessions_spawn`
2. Allow the child to perform a longer-running task until its backing session disappears
3. Observe the child CLI task becomes `lost` with `backing session missing`
4. Observe the parent subagent task remains `running` and the `task_mirrored` TaskFlow remains `running`
5. Run `openclaw tasks audit --json` — parent task and TaskFlow reported as `stale_running`
6. Restart the gateway — records remain inconsistent
7. **Before this PR**: `openclaw tasks maintenance --apply` does not reconcile the parent subagent or the TaskFlow
8. **After this PR**: `runTaskRegistryMaintenance` detects the terminal backing child via the shared `runId`, marks the parent subagent as `lost`, and the TaskFlow is automatically terminalized

### Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed (92285)**: Task maintenance fails to reconcile stale subagent parent tasks whose backing CLI children are already terminal. The parent stays `running`, and the `task_mirrored` TaskFlow stays `running`, despite the backing session being missing. After this fix, the maintenance sweep detects the terminal backing child (matched by shared `runId`) and marks the parent subagent as `lost`, which triggers automatic TaskFlow terminalization via the existing `syncFlowFromTaskAfterTaskMutation` hook.

**Real environment tested**: Linux, Node 22 — Vitest with `runTaskRegistryMaintenance` against the task-registry maintenance harness (real in-memory task store, real maintenance loop, real session lookup context)

**Exact steps or command run after this patch**: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/tasks/task-registry.maintenance.issue-60299.test.ts src/tasks/task-flow-registry.maintenance.test.ts`

**Evidence after fix**:
```text
[test] starting test/vitest/vitest.unit-fast.config.ts
 RUN  v4.1.7 /home/0668001395/openclawProject1
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  5 passed (5)

[test] starting test/vitest/vitest.tasks.config.ts
 RUN  v4.1.7 /home/0668001395/openclawProject1
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  20 passed (20)

[test] passed 2 Vitest shards in 22.01s
```

**Observed result after fix**: The new test case "reconciles stale subagent tasks whose backing CLI child is already terminal (regresses #92285)" creates a `lost` CLI child and a `running` subagent parent sharing the same `runId`, then runs maintenance. The parent subagent task transitions from `running` to `lost` with error `"backing session missing"`, and the maintenance summary reports 1 reconciled task. All 19 existing tests in the same file continue to pass — no regression in cron recovery, ACP reclamation, subagent recovery wedged detection, or task pruning behavior. The TaskFlow maintenance tests (5 tests) also pass unchanged, confirming the existing flow-terminalization path is preserved.

**What was not tested**: A live Gateway round-trip with a real full-agent subagent orchestration spawning a CLI child that becomes lost was not driven. The maintenance harness uses a real in-memory task store and the real maintenance loop, but the backing-session-lookup context is synthetic. The intermittent child-loss trigger itself (which depends on real process termination) was not reproduced.

**Repro confirmation**: The new test case fails without the fix — the subagent parent task stays `running` after maintenance because `hasBackingSession` returns `true` for the childless subagent. With the fix, the parent is reconciled to `lost`, and the linked `task_mirrored` flow is terminalized by the existing `syncFlowFromTaskAfterTaskMutation` hook.

### Risk / Mitigation

- **Risk**: Expanding the maintenance sweep iteration by one additional `Set` pre-computation pass could increase sweep latency.
  **Mitigation**: The pre-computation is a single O(n) pass over the same `tasks` array already held in memory — no additional disk I/O or store reads. On a typical task ledger of a few hundred entries, this adds negligible overhead (< 1ms).
- **Risk**: The `terminalRunIds.has(current.runId)` check could false-positive if a different task type shares the same `runId` by coincidence.
  **Mitigation**: The check is gated on `current.runtime === "subagent"`, `current.status === "running"`, no `childSessionKey`, and `hasLostGraceExpired`. subagent tasks share `runId` with their backing CLI child by design (the orchestrator assigns matching runIds). A coincidental runId collision across unrelated tasks would require an identical UUID — effectively impossible.
- **Risk**: A subagent task whose backing CLI child was terminalized for a different reason (e.g., manual cancellation) could be incorrectly marked `lost` instead of `cancelled`.
  **Mitigation**: `markTaskLost` preserves the original task's error field. The backing child's terminal error (`"backing session missing"`) is propagated. If the child was cancelled, its status would be `cancelled` not `lost`, and its runId would be in `terminalRunIds` regardless — but the resulting parent status of `lost` with a descriptive error is a safe terminal state that unblocks the orchestrator. Future refinement could propagate the child's exact terminal status.

### Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix

### Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] tasks (task registry maintenance)

### Regression Test Plan

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/tasks/task-registry.maintenance.issue-60299.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/tasks/task-flow-registry.maintenance.test.ts`

### Review Findings Addressed

N/A (initial submission)

### Linked Issue/PR

Fixes #92285

target.title: fix(telegram): record sent messages in the source-delivery path
target.body:
## Summary

- The Telegram source-delivery path (`deliverReplies` in `extensions/telegram/src/bot/delivery.replies.ts`) sends via `sendTelegramText` / raw media APIs and emits the `message_sent` hook, but never appends to the sent-message ledger.
- The canonical `sendMessageTelegram` (`extensions/telegram/src/send.ts`) still calls `recordSentMessage` after each send; the recording was lost when this delivery path was split out into the `delivery.*` modules, so automatic source-delivery silently stops populating the ledger.
- Impact: `wasSentByBot` returns false for the bot's own replies, reaction filters mis-fire, and reply-to routing that depends on the ledger misses legitimate sends.
- Outcome: append the delivered message to the ledger in lockstep with the `message_sent` hook (same success gate and message id), keeping hook and ledger in sync.
- Out of scope: per-chunk recording of multi-chunk replies — the `message_sent` hook itself reports only the first delivered id per reply, and this change matches that granularity.
- Reviewers: confirm the recording belongs co-located with the `message_sent` emission (vs inside each low-level send helper), and that the success gate matches.

## Linked context

Closes #92242

Related: `extensions/telegram/src/send.ts` `recordSentMessage` calls (the canonical path that still records); `extensions/telegram/src/sent-message-cache.ts` (`recordSentMessage` / `wasSentByBot`).

Requested by a maintainer or owner? No — filed against open issue #92242.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: source-delivery emits `message_sent` but never records into the sent-message ledger, so `wasSentByBot` is always false for the bot's own replies (#92242).
- Real environment tested: Linux, Node 24, this repo checkout. Ran the real `deliverReplies` against the real `sent-message-cache` (`extensions/telegram/src/sent-message-cache.ts`) with a stub Telegram Bot whose `api.sendMessage` returns a `message_id`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node node_modules/tsx/dist/cli.mjs proof-92242.mts` — calls `wasSentByBot(chatId, messageId, cfg)` before and after a `deliverReplies` text send.
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output): copied live console output (stdout) from the real delivery + ledger:

```
ledger wasSentByBot BEFORE delivery -> false
deliverReplies result -> {"delivered":true}
ledger wasSentByBot AFTER  delivery -> true
```

- Observed result after fix: the ledger is empty before delivery, the send reports `delivered: true`, and `wasSentByBot` returns true afterward — the bot's own message is now recognized as self-sent.
- What was not tested: a full live gateway → Telegram round trip against the Telegram Bot API.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: this checkout has no live Telegram bot token / chat, so the send target is a stub `bot.api.sendMessage`; everything else (the real `deliverReplies` delivery flow and the real `sent-message-cache` ledger read/write) runs unmocked.
- Before evidence: the `BEFORE` line above shows the ledger empty prior to delivery; on `main` it stays empty after delivery too (no `recordSentMessage` call exists in the delivery path).

## Tests and validation

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run extensions/telegram/src/bot/delivery.records-sent-message.test.ts` → 2 passed (new file: records on success, skips when nothing delivered).
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run extensions/telegram/src/bot/delivery.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/send.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot/delivery.resolve-media-retry.test.ts` → 195 passed (regression).
- `oxfmt --check` clean on both changed files.
- What failed before: with the pre-fix delivery path, `recordSentMessage` was never called (the new test fails red against `main`).

## Risk checklist

- Did user-visible behavior change? Indirectly — reaction filtering / reply-to routing / `wasSentByBot` now work for source-delivered replies; no change to message content.
- Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? No.
- Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? No.
- Highest-risk area: double-recording or recording on failure.
- Mitigation: the call is gated on the same `progress.deliveredCount > deliveredCountBeforeReply` success condition as the `message_sent` hook and a non-null message id, and runs once per reply at the same chokepoint.

## Current review state

- Next action: maintainer review.
- Waiting on: review; no known author blockers.
- Bot/reviewer comments addressed: none yet.

target.title: fix(llm): collapse cumulative openai-responses message snapshots instead of concatenating [AI-assisted]
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #91959 — Bedrock Mantle's `openai-responses` endpoint (GPT-5.x with reasoning enabled) re-emits the assistant message as many **cumulative snapshot items**, each a strict prefix-superset of the previous one, instead of a single final message item. The corruption is server-side (confirmed in the issue via raw `curl`, streaming and non-streaming, usage billed once), but both OpenClaw stream consumers appended one text block per item, so the final visible reply, the session transcript, and the replay context repeated the answer once per snapshot (observed 49–80×).

This adds a generic rule in the shared compat seam (`resolveResponsesMessageSnapshotCollapse`): a message item whose text strictly extends the **immediately preceding** text block, with a matching phase, replaces that block (last-wins); identical or shrinking same-phase snapshots are dropped. The first item's `textSignature` is kept so replay and stream-item identity stay stable. Any non-message output item (reasoning, tool call) resets the tracking, so genuinely distinct post-tool messages (e.g. repeated Codex commentary around tool calls) are never merged and reasoning items are never orphaned for replay. Different-phase (`commentary`/`final_answer`) items keep today's separate-block behavior. Applied in the agent transport stream, the shared LLM consumer, and the completed-response backfill; no provider-name keying.

Known scope cut: if a snapshot-emitting provider also re-streams the prefix as `output_text.delta` events, streaming *previews* can still transiently show duplicated text mid-stream (downstream phase-aware rendering self-heals at item boundaries); the canonical message, final visible text, transcript, and replay are correct.

AI-assisted (Claude Code), directed and reviewed by the PR author. Session logs available on request.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: A real agent turn against a Responses stream shaped exactly like the corrupted Bedrock Mantle output returned the answer repeated once per snapshot in `finalAssistantVisibleText` (#91959).

Real environment tested: macOS (Darwin 25.5.0), Node 22.22.1, real CLI end-to-end: `openclaw agent --local` (embedded agent runner, real openai-responses transport, real session store) on a scratch `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR`, with a custom provider (`api: "openai-responses"`, `baseUrl: http://127.0.0.1:38291/v1`) backed by a local mock server that replays the corrupted wire shape documented in the issue: a reasoning item followed by 4 cumulative `message` snapshot items (each its own `output_item.added` / `output_text.delta` / `output_item.done` sequence, `phase: "final_answer"`, strictly prefix-increasing), then `response.completed` with single-copy usage. Live Bedrock Mantle was not reachable from this environment; the wire shape follows the issue's raw SSE captures.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
1. `node mantle-mock-server.mjs` (serves the corrupted SSE stream on `127.0.0.1:38291`)
2. `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=/tmp/openclaw-91959-proof openclaw agent --local --session-key proof-91959 --model mantlemock/gpt-5.5-mock --json -m "Explain the Transformer self-attention mechanism."`
3. Inspect `meta.finalAssistantVisibleText`: length and occurrence count of the opening phrase.

Evidence after fix: copied live output — terminal output (stdout) from the real `openclaw agent --local` runs, inspecting `meta.finalAssistantVisibleText` of the run's `--json` result.

Before (same command, same mock stream, the three touched source files from `origin/main`):

```
visible text length: 696
opening-phrase occurrences: 4
--- first 400 chars ---
Self-attention projects each token into Q, K and V vectors.
Self-attention projects each token into Q, K and V vectors. Scaled dot-product attention divides QK^T by sqrt(d_k) before the softmax.
Self-attention projects each token into Q, K and V vectors. Scaled dot-product attention divides QK^T by sqrt(d_k) before the softmax. Multi-head attention runs several projections in parallel and concaten
```

After (this patch):

```
visible text length: 280
opening-phrase occurrences: 1
--- text ---
Self-attention projects each token into Q, K and V vectors. Scaled dot-product attention divides QK^T by sqrt(d_k) before the softmax. Multi-head attention runs several projections in parallel and concatenates the heads. Positional encodings such as RoPE inject order information.
```

Observed result after fix: the reply contains the answer a single time; the persisted assistant message holds one text block carrying the first snapshot item's signature.

What was not tested: live Bedrock Mantle (no AWS credentials in this environment — the server-side corruption itself is already evidenced in the issue by raw `curl` transcripts from @DaiMingNJ); GPT-5.4-shaped variants (`replayInvalid=true` runs); multi-turn replay against a live provider after a collapsed turn; channel streaming previews during the corrupted stream (covered by code reading only, see scope cut above).

## Verification

- `pnpm test src/llm/**` (24 files, 227 tests) and `src/agents/openai-transport-stream.test.ts` + responses replay/payload/subscriber suites (31 files, ~400 tests): all green after rebase onto `origin/main` (d4819948f37).
- New regression tests: cumulative-snapshot collapse (streaming + backfill, both consumers), repeated-snapshot drop, and negatives pinning the boundaries — different phases, a reasoning item between snapshots, and a tool call between prefix-nested messages must NOT collapse. The collapse tests fail without the fix (verified by reverting the three source files).
- `pnpm build` green; `tsgo` core + core-test lanes green; oxlint/oxfmt clean; `pnpm check:import-cycles` green (0 cycles).
- `pnpm check:changed` delegates to Blacksmith Testbox, which is not reachable from this environment (crabbox binary sanity check fails locally) — relying on PR CI for the canonical changed gates.
- Local `codex review --base origin/main` hit the account usage limit (resets 11:41 AM local); a multi-agent adversarial review pass (7 finder angles + verification) was run instead, and its main finding — the first iteration could collapse across reasoning/tool-call boundaries, risking dropped post-tool messages and orphaned reasoning items in replay — is fixed and pinned by the negative tests above.

Release-note context: user-facing fix — replies through `openai-responses` providers that emit cumulative message snapshots (observed: Bedrock Mantle with GPT-5.x reasoning) no longer repeat the answer dozens of times; transcripts and replay context stay clean. Surface: `src/agents/openai-transport-stream.ts`, `src/llm/providers/openai-responses-shared.ts`, `src/shared/openai-responses-stream-compat.ts`. Reported by @phoenixyy with server-side evidence from @DaiMingNJ in #91959.

target.title: fix(cron): bound restart catch-up to schedule activation
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #91944.

Cron restart catch-up now replays only schedule slots that occurred after the schedule was activated. The scheduler records `state.scheduleActivatedAtMs` when a job is created or when its schedule/enabled state changes, and both startup catch-up and error-backoff replay use that boundary.

Existing cron rows are handled by `openclaw doctor --fix`: the cron preflight first imports legacy cron state, then transactionally stamps rows missing the activation field with the migration time. Runtime reads only the canonical field and fails closed when it is absent, so an upgrade cannot infer and send stale pre-upgrade outbound slots.

Metadata-only edits do not move the boundary. Public create/update inputs cannot inject scheduler-owned activation state.

## Verification

- 146 focused cron, doctor, protocol, and service tests passed locally.
- Targeted oxfmt and oxlint passed.
- Azure Linux Crabbox focused proof passed: lease `cbx_4ce772051720`, run `run_6f4988261933`, 120 tests.
- Fresh Codex autoreview: no actionable findings, patch correct at 0.91 confidence.
- Exact-head GitHub CI is running on `0e30df1f5a45d4a83d095e6cdf559db1ee05eda6`.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: An API-updated cron schedule must not replay a slot that predates the schedule activation after a gateway restart.

Real environment tested: The contributor ran a real macOS Gateway with a scratch SQLite cron store, created and edited the job through the real CLI/Gateway RPC path, injected the historical prior-schedule run, and restarted the Gateway. The final repaired head was additionally validated on Azure Linux Crabbox against the focused cron/doctor/SQLite suites.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Create the cron job through `openclaw cron add`, change its expression through `openclaw cron edit`, set the prior-schedule `lastRunAtMs` in the scratch SQLite row, restart the Gateway, and inspect the job state and missed-job logs. For the final doctor-owned legacy path, remove `scheduleActivatedAtMs`, run the cron doctor preflight migration, and verify the canonical activation boundary is written before restart evaluation.

Evidence after fix:

```text
real Gateway, activation field present:
  nextRunAtMs: 2026-07-11T14:18:00.000Z (unchanged after restart)
  lastRunAtMs: 2026-05-11T07:18:00.000Z (unchanged; job did not fire)
  new missed-job log lines: 0

final repaired head:
  Azure Linux lease: cbx_4ce772051720
  run: run_6f4988261933
  focused cron/doctor/SQLite tests: 120 passed
  doctor migration transactionally stamps only cron rows missing scheduleActivatedAtMs
  runtime does not infer a legacy activation boundary
```

Observed result after fix: The updated job remains on its future API-selected schedule after restart. Existing rows receive one conservative doctor-owned activation boundary, preventing stale pre-upgrade outbound replay.

What was not tested: A full version-to-version `openclaw update` install flow. The real Gateway restart covered the field-present runtime path; the final legacy migration replacement is covered by current-head SQLite integration tests and the Azure Linux run, so a maintainer proof override is applied for that portion.

## Risk

This changes restart delivery behavior and persisted cron state. The conservative migration may skip a pre-upgrade missed slot, but it will not send an inferred stale outbound job. No config or environment surface is added.

Release-note context: cron jobs no longer fire slots that predate their current schedule activation after a gateway restart or upgrade. Reported by @suifatt7799-oss in #91944.
